---
title: Quarto profiles
---

Profiles let a Quarto project reuse source documents while changing render
settings for a particular audience or product.

## Recommended profile names

New teaching-material projects should use `student` for learner-facing output
and `instructor` for answer-bearing output. These names align with the
`teaching.show-solutions` metadata used by the
[`quarto-teaching-tools`](https://github.com/au-mbg/quarto-teaching-tools)
versioning extensions.

## Base configuration

Install `callout-solution` and/or `strip-solution` and list the filters used
by the project in `_quarto.yml`:

```yaml
filters:
  - callout-solution
  - strip-solution
```

`callout-solution` is appropriate for instructor-only answer callouts;
`strip-solution` is appropriate when student and instructor versions select
different divs or code cells.

## Profile files

The profile files select output destinations and set the explicit solution
visibility switch:

```yaml
# _quarto-student.yml
project:
  output-dir: _output/student

teaching:
  show-solutions: false
```

```yaml
# _quarto-instructor.yml
project:
  output-dir: _output/instructor

teaching:
  show-solutions: true
  default-solution-title: "Solution"
```

Define Pixi tasks that pass the selected profile to Quarto:

```toml
[tasks.preview]
args = [{ "arg" = "profile", default = "student",
          choices = ["student", "instructor"] }]
cmd = "quarto preview --profile {{profile}}"
cwd = "course"

[tasks.render]
args = [{ "arg" = "profile", default = "student",
          choices = ["student", "instructor"] }]
cmd = "quarto render --profile {{profile}}"
cwd = "course"
```

Contributors can then run commands such as `pixi run preview student` or
`pixi run render instructor`.

Set `teaching.show-solutions` explicitly in both profiles. The
`callout-solution` extension can recognize a profile name containing
`solution` as a fallback, but that is not the recommended configuration for
new projects.

## Existing naming patterns

Different courses use `exercise`/`solution`, `student`/`instructor`, or
`student`/`master`. Maintain these existing projects using their configured
task names and filters; use `student`/`instructor` for new projects.

## When profiles matter

Render all affected profiles when changing:

- Answer text or instructor guidance.
- Learner starter code or executable solution cells.
- Links between variants or shared navigation.
- Profile-dependent format output such as notebooks or PDFs.

Profile behavior relies on extension configuration and metadata as well as
the YAML profile file, so investigate all configured parts before changing
the visibility model.
